channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Insurity’s Cassiopeia Release Expands AI Capabilities
item.description: Insurity says Cassiopeia also expands APIs, embedded insurance capabilities and automated data sharing to support integrations and partner ecosystem connectivity. (Image source: Insurity.) Cassiopeia, the latest platform release from Insurity (Hartford, Conn.), adds AI-guided decision support, expanded automation, compliance controls and ecosystem connectivity across insurance operations. Our latest release is designed to help customers accelerate their operations using AI without losing visibility into the decisions, processes, and data that drive their business.” Insurity says Cassiopeia reflects its focus on practical operational enhancements that help insurers increase agility while maintaining oversight across insurance workflows.

item.title: Weighing anchor is easier said than done
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: AI Drives Another Spree Of Megadeals
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . startup funding rounds centered around the sector one would suspect: artificial intelligence.

item.title: Nuevos gobiernos en Colombia y Perú: las prioridades del sector fintech
item.description: Definir reglas para el mercado cripto en Colombia y crear un súper sandbox de innovación en Perú, estas son las prioridades que desde el sector fintech se plantea a los nuevos gobiernos que asumirán este año en ambos países. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Revolut cambia, Yape expande y Pix vuelve al foco
item.description: Revolut México ajusta condiciones de rendimiento Revolut modificó los requisitos para acceder al rendimiento anual de 15% que lanzó con su entrada al mercado mexicano. Los clientes deberán realizar al menos cuatro compras mensuales o contratar un plan de pago para conservar la tasa promocional, en un ajuste de su estrategia inicial para captar usuarios. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Americans Are Inundated With Scams. Why Do So Few Victims Report Them?
item.description: Most Americans are inundated with scam attempts on a daily basis — and about 3 in 10 have personally lost money or personal information to scams, according to a new AP-NORC survey. Many victims didn’t report the scam, Gallup found, because they didn’t think it would make a difference in getting money back. A separate survey conducted by Gallup and the Stop Scams Alliance that was provided exclusively to the AP found that last year alone, about 1 in 10 U.S.

item.title: Deadly Neglect Lawsuits, Fines Follow Ohio Nursing Home Chain
item.description: “It’s one of those things where he should have never gotten a bedsore to begin with.” The lawsuit Hill filed notes that Ray’s death is similar to that of a woman named Lucy Garcia, a patient at the Arbors at Oregon, a nearby nursing home in the same chain. A pressure ulcer slowly tore open a hole in the skin over Sam Frank Ray’s tailbone, leaving his raw bone exposed for weeks to the infection that eventually killed him, according to a lawsuit his family filed late last year. Bedsores, the byproduct of cut-off blood circulation when the body lies still in one position for too long, are considered highly preventable injuries.

item.title: Few Protections Against Workplace Surveillance for Canadian Workers as TD Starts Monitoring
item.description: Canadian employees have few legal protections against workplace monitoring, experts said, after TD Bank told some staff it would use software to track their work activity, raising concerns about consent and privacy. Canada’s privacy laws are weaker than those in the European Union, where privacy is a fundamental right, and companies must justify the use of monitoring tools under privacy and labor laws, according to six Canadian legal experts. There are limits, however, to the reasons employers monitor their workers and how they use the data they collect.

item.title: California’s Major Fault Intersection at Highest Stress Level in 1000 Years: Study
item.description: Southern California’s major fault system is more stressed than at any point in the last 1,000 years, according to a new study by the University of Bern. Liliane Burkhard of the Division of Space Research and Planetary Sciences (WP) at the Physics Institute of the University of Bern examined 1,000 years of earthquake activity along the southern San Andreas and San Jacinto fault systems to estimate current stress levels at Cajon Pass. Since the magnitude 7.9 Fort Tejon earthquake struck in 1857, stress has continued to accumulate along these fault segments, researchers found.

item.title: Microstructure, not correlation, shields pure-play cat bond ETFs from deleveraging regimes: King Ridge’s Pagnani
item.description: However, a new paper from Rick Pagnani, CEO of specialised insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment manager King Ridge Capital LLC, argues that this assumption is both analytically and structurally flawed when measured against the actual mechanics of the cat bond market. Recall in late December 2025, King Ridge Capital Advisors launched its UCITS exchange traded catastrophe bond fund (ETF) in partnership with UCITS ETF white-label platform provider HANetf, with London-based specialist Goldenberg Hehmeyer LLP (GHCO), as its designated lead market maker. In the paper, Pagnani notes that while cat bond ETFs have not yet weathered a full stress cycle since their recent introduction, historical data from the underlying catastrophe bond market provides a clear framework for predicting how these ETFs will behave.

item.title: New Windmill III Re cat bond continues Achmea Re’s strategy to diversify reinsurance: MD Bom
item.description: For Achmea Re, the successful completion of its new €100 million Windmill III Re DAC (2026-1) catastrophe bond sponsorship is a continuation of its strategy to diversify its sources of reinsurance and strengthen its relationships with capital market investors, according to Managing Director Ewoud Bom. Ewoud Bom, managing director of Achmea Reinsurance said, “This Windmill III Re Series 2026-1 issuance is a continuation of our strategy to diversify our access to reinsurance capacity worldwide and to broaden and strengthen our relationships with capital market investors. “We believe in the mutual benefit of our strategy to transfer part of our risk to the capital market, which is confirmed by the good reception from investors of our fifth issuance.” Achmea Re said the new cat bond forms part of its overall catastrophe reinsurance purchase, diversifying its sources of protection against catastrophe events, providing multi-year cover running from July 2026 to the end of June 2030.

item.title: AM Best reports split in US cyber insurance market as losses and pricing pressures shift
item.description: AM Best, the insurance credit rating agency and analytics provider, has observed that the US cyber insurance sector has effectively separated into two distinct sub-markets. According to AM Best’s latest findings, total cyber insurance premiums in the US edged up to $7.5 billion in 2025 from $7.1 billion a year earlier. Fred Eslami, Associate Director at AM Best, explained: “What’s important to note here is that for several years now, large companies, with mature captive insurers and sophisticated cyber risk mitigation practices, have moved away, in part or entirely, from the commercial market and insured their cyber exposure via their captives.

item.title: Achmea Reinsurance secures €100m of European windstorm & severe thunderstorm retro with latest cat bond
item.description: has returned to the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market with a €100 million Windmill III Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond, providing cover for European windstorm and severe thunderstorm losses on an ultimate net loss, per-occurrence basis. This transaction marks Achmea Reinsurance’s fifth sponsored cat bond and its third under Rule 144A of the US Securities Act. Achmea Reinsurance has entered into a four-year reinsurance agreement with Windmill III Re DAC, a multi-arrangement special purpose vehicle incorporated in Ireland, providing per-occurrence retrocession cover for losses originating from its non-life insurance companies, Achmea Schadeverzekeringen N.V.

item.title: Lemonade enters into $250m business financing agreement with Hannover Re
item.description: Lemonade, an AI-powered digital insurance company, has signed a business financing agreement with Hannover Re (Ireland) DAC, under which the reinsurer will provide up to $250 million to help fund Lemonade’s sales and marketing spending between January 1, 2027, and December 31, 2028. This agreement with Hannover Re is essentially an extension of Lemonade’s Synthetic Agents strategy as the firm targets capital-efficient growth. At the beginning of each monthly Growth Period, Hannover Re can fund up to 80% of Lemonade’s sales and marketing costs, with a limit of $20 million per Reference Cohort.

item.title: Venezuela Seeks Quake Survivors as Death Toll Hits 589
item.description: The death toll from twin earthquakes that rocked Venezuela rose to at least 589 with nearly 3,000 more injured, as rescue teams desperately clear away rubble to reach trapped victims. Venezuela’s political opposition has set up a set up a website to track missing people in parallel to government efforts and it shows that there are more than 58,000 people unaccounted for. In emailed comments, reinsurance broker Howden Re said the quake is the latest reminder of a structural challenge facing the reinsurance industry globally.

item.title: A Super Yacht Armada Came to Miami, Leaving a Marine Graveyard in Its Wake
item.description: Not far from the super yachts docked outside the island palaces of Miami’s new billionaire class, smaller boats abandoned by people who couldn’t afford them languish in Biscayne Bay. “A lot of people buy a boat but don’t realize how much it costs for fuel, maintenance and marina fees,” said Sergeant Javier Fernandez, commander of the Miami Beach police marine unit, as he surveyed the abandoned vessels about 100 yards off Star Island aboard the department’s 32-foot Contender center console patrol boat. The marine graveyard is one downside of an unprecedented surge in boating since the pandemic, spurred in part by an influx of well-to-do newcomers.

item.title: Appellate Court Rejects EPA Bid to Ease Regulations for Coal-Fired Power Plants
item.description: federal appeals court on Friday rejected the Environmental Protection Agency’s bid to withdraw Biden-era limits on soot pollution from coal-fired power plants and factories, in a setback for the Trump administration’s deregulatory efforts. The EPA under President Donald Trump last year asked the appeals court to invalidate the 2024 rule, arguing the agency had acted unreasonably by failing to consider costs while setting the standard. The court rejected the petition, leaving the annual limit of 9 micrograms per cubic meter of fine particulate matter, known as PM 2.5, in place.

item.title: AXA XL Appoints Jeanmarie Giordano as Global Chief Underwriting Officer
item.description: AXA XL has appointed Jeanmarie Giordano as Global Chief Underwriting Officer, reinforcing its leadership team as the insurer continues to focus on disciplined underwriting, portfolio resilience and sustainable growth across its global business. Giordano joins AXA XL from Everest, where she most recently served as North America Chief Underwriting Officer, a role she assumed in March 2025. She brings more than two decades of underwriting and leadership experience to the role, including nearly 15 years at AIG, where she held several senior leadership positions, including Global Head of Multinational for Financial Lines and Chief Underwriting Officer – Professional Liability.

item.title: 3 Key Questions for Boards on AI
item.description: Boards approving AI deployments often miss critical questions about data foundations, decision documentation, and pipeline positioning. What has not gone up enough is the quality of the questions board members ask before approving these proposals. Across 20 years of building insurance software and watching deployments succeed or fail, I've seen three other questions matter more for the long-term success of an AI decision-making deployment.

item.title: CFC adds affirmative AI coverage across insurance portfolio
item.description: Specialist insurer CFC has added affirmative AI coverage language to seven insurance products, introducing explicit wording for AI-related exposures such as model hallucinations, AI-generated content, and model drift. While much of the market discussion around AI has centred on cyber insurance, we have taken a broader approach that integrates coverage across multiple products, reflecting the true breadth of its impact on business risk. Rather than launching a standalone AI product, CFC is embedding AI considerations across multiple lines, viewing AI as an accelerant of existing risks.

item.title: Aviva launches digital Contractors Combined product
item.description: Aviva has launched a Contractors Combined proposition aimed at small and mid-sized construction businesses, expanding the range of construction risks that brokers can place online. Available on Acturis and soon on Aviva’s Fast Trade platform, the modular policy allows brokers to quote and bind Contractors Combined cover online. “Brokers have told us they want to place more Contractors Combined business online – not just liability, but a broader mix of risks.

item.title: La DGSFP recaba información a las aseguradoras sobre su encaje en el nuevo marco de proporcionalidad de Solvencia II
item.description: La DGSFP ha publicado en su página web un cuestionario dirigido a entidades aseguradoras y reaseguradoras para recopilar información sobre cómo afectará a su organización el nuevo marco de proporcionalidad introducido por la revisión de la Directiva Solvencia II (Directiva (UE) 2025/2).

item.title: PIB Group Iberia nombra a Fernando Franco director de las unidades de Crédito y Caución
item.description: Fernando Franco ha sido nombrado director de las unidades de Crédito y Caución de PIB Group en el marco de una reorganización orientada a reforzar su capacidad de asesoramiento en riesgos financieros para empresas.

item.title: El ratio combinado en Autos RC sigue descontrolado
item.description: La mejora que desde hace unos meses están experimentando los resultados en el seguro de Autos oculta el todavía deficiente desempeño técnico de la cobertura de RC Obligatoria.

item.title: Traffic Flows Through Hormuz Despite Shock Ship Attack
item.description: An attack on a container vessel sailing through the Strait of Hormuz has prompted some shipowners to review exit plans, but traffic continued to flow in both directions through the vital thoroughfare on Friday. UK Maritime Trade Operations said in an update that the US had facilitated 80 ship crossings through Hormuz over June 23 and 24. After the US-Iran peace deal, Western navies have recommended using the southern route that tracks Oman’s coast, but on Thursday a container ship called Ever Lovely was hit while using the corridor, the first attack since the interim peace agreement was signed.

item.title: Big I: Independent Agencies’ Market Share Up Slightly in 2025
item.description: According to the 2026 Market Share Report published by the Independent Insurance Agents & Brokers of America (Big “I”) trade association, independent agents (IAs) wrote 87.7% of commercial lines written premiums in 2025 and 39.5% of personal lines. “The resilience of the independent agency channel is evident in this year’s Market Share Report, painting a clear picture of its stability through the hard market,” says Charles Symington, Big “I” president & CEO. in 2025, a slight increase from 2024 when 61.5% of insurance written was placed by independent agents.

item.title: U.S. Supreme Court Scales Back Roundup Cancer Lawsuits
item.description: Supreme Court reined in thousands of lawsuits pursued in state courts accusing Bayer of failing to warn users that the active ingredient in its Roundup weedkiller causes cancer, handing a major legal victory on Thursday to the German company. Bayer said before the Supreme Court ruled that a decision in its favor could largely end the Roundup litigation. The Supreme Court agreed with Bayer that a U.S.

item.title: Liberty, Howden Re, Markel, HDI: 14 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: PartnerRe | Dementia the protection gap insurers can no longer ignore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA consults on targeted changes to listing rules for closed-ended investment funds
item.description: The FCA has published a consultation paper on proposed changes to its UK Listing Rules for closed‑ended investment funds, focused on the management of conflicts of interest. Closed‑ended investment funds have a distinct structure, operating as both listed companies and investment vehicles. These proposals are targeted, forward-looking changes to how conflicts of interest are managed, reflecting the central role of the investment management relationship for these companies.

item.title: 6 Startup Investors On What It Will Take To Fund More Black Founders
item.description: What the numbers don’t tell us is why investors continue to overlook those entrepreneurs, and more importantly, how the industry can improve the odds for Black and other underrepresented business leaders. To better understand what’s driving the persistent gap — and what it will take to close it — Crunchbase News spoke with six venture capitalists who actively back Black founders about where they believe the ecosystem continues to fall short and how it can improve outcomes. Part 1 explored the data on funding to Black founders, and in Part 2 we spoke with Black founders who became investors.

item.title: Is the cat bond market truly mainstream yet? Artemis Bermuda Roundtable 2026
item.description: The extent to whether catastrophe bonds have truly become a mainstream investment product was a key discussion point during Artemis’ 2026 Bermuda ILS Executive Roundtable , in which experts from across the ILS sector discussed whether rated bonds, pooled ILS offerings, and enhanced secondary liquidity are ready to unlock vast new sources of traditional capital. In partnership with Appleby and Moody’s , Artemis held its seventh Bermuda insurance-linked securities (ILS) executive roundtable in Hamilton in April, bringing together leaders from across the expanding sector to discuss current market trends and future opportunities. During the roundtable, participants discussed how it feels like the cat bond market has reached somewhat of an inflection point, in terms of having had such strong performance over the last few years.

item.title: $75m Woody Re 2026-1 cat bond priced for Fidelis Partnership Syndicate 3123
item.description: The now priced $75 million of Woody Re 2026-1 Class A cat bond notes are designed to provide the Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s with a source of catastrophe reinsurance against losses from named storms, earthquakes, severe thunderstorms, winter storms and wildfire events in part of North America. The Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s is set to benefit from its first catastrophe bond protection, as the Arthur Re Ltd. As we explained, it will be the first catastrophe bond that will provide reinsurance protection to Syndicate 3123, the Lloyd’s structure launched in 2024 by the Fidelis Partnership in collaboration with capacity partner Pelagos Insurance Capital (formerly Fidelis Insurance) .

item.title: July Casualty and Financial Lines renewals orderly as reinsurers reward stronger portfolios: Howden Re
item.description: A new Howden Re report focused on the 1 July 2026 Casualty and Financial lines renewals has suggested that for those willing to look past the noise and into the data, there is a compelling case for profitable growth in the market. The carriers who are succeeding are making educated decisions about where the returns are.” In the face of this market dynamic, the report said that the 1 July Casualty and Financial Lines reinsurance renewals were completed in an orderly fashion. “As property pricing softens and fresh capital continues to flow into the P&C market, the US Casualty and Financial Lines market is grappling with the question of how to grow with sustainable returns in a business that is increasingly defined by performance dispersion,” Howden Re observed in its new report.

item.title: Insured losses from Venezuela quakes likely to be a ‘fraction’ of total multi-billion economic loss: Aon
item.description: Insurance industry losses related to the two major earthquakes that hit Venezuela this week are anticipated to represent only a small portion of the billions of dollars in economic losses expected from this catastrophe, according to broking group Aon’s recent Weekly Cat Report. According to the report, the tremors occurring on June 24 2026 will likely become the deadliest in Venezuela, at least since 1812, when a major tremor killed approximately 26,000 people. Northwestern Venezuela, specifically the area west of Caracas, was recently impacted by two successive strike-slip earthquakes with magnitudes of 7.5 and 7.2.

item.title: Fitch expects Japanese non-life Insurers to sustain strong underwriting momentum
item.description: Fitch Ratings, the international credit ratings agency, believes Japan’s leading non-life insurers are well placed to continue delivering strong underwriting results. Looking ahead, Fitch expects underwriting performance to remain resilient despite lower anticipated gains from strategic shareholding disposals. According to Fitch, Japan’s three largest non-life insurance groups – MS&AD Insurance Group Holdings, Inc.

item.title: Mamdani Delivers Rent Freeze in Milestone for New York City Tenants
item.description: New York City’s Rent Guidelines Board voted to freeze rents for roughly one million stabilized apartments, handing Mayor Zohran Mamdani a major political victory and delivering on a central promise that launched him to City Hall. The party atmosphere continued after the vote, with the crowd singing, “we are the tenants,” to the tune of Queen’s “We Are The Champions.” Mamdani called the vote a historic victory for New York City tenants. Mamdani has repeatedly called for a freeze, arguing that stabilized tenants are facing rising costs for food, transportation and other necessities even as wages fail to keep pace.

item.title: Why the Trump Administration Is Defending Musk’s xAI Data Centers in Court
item.description: Burnham has previously served as legal chief of the Department of Government Efficiency, as well as held senior roles in the White House and Justice Department during Trump’s first administration. The outcome of the case not only has implications for other data centers, which have already been resorting to unconventional power sources to avoid long lines to be connected to the grid, but other types of polluters potentially looking to skimp on pollution controls. A lawsuit over unpermitted gas turbines used to power adata center for Elon Musk’s company xAI Corp.

item.title: PetGPT launches pet insurance comparison app in ChatGPT
item.description: PetGPT, a new app available within ChatGPT, allows US dog and cat owners to compare pet insurance quotes from multiple providers without leaving the AI platform. Beyond price comparisons, PetGPT also provides information about breed-specific health conditions and typical treatment costs, helping pet owners better understand the value of different insurance options. We tried quoting pet insurance for both a dog and a cat using the website version, and in both cases the quotes displayed were from Lemonade, Spot, and Fetch .

item.title: GUARD launches contractors package
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway GUARD has launched a Contractors General Liability and Inland Marine insurance solution for artisan contractors, expanding its small commercial product lineup. The offering is designed for contractors that do not maintain a permanent physical location and primarily work at third-party sites and job locations. The product is initially available in Arizona, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Minnesota, and Pennsylvania, with additional state expansions planned later this year.

item.title: Pharmacists Mutual launches PM Healthcare Protect
item.description: Pharmacists Mutual launched PM Healthcare Protect, a new product brand offering commercial insurance solutions for healthcare professionals and business owners. Founded in 1909 and headquartered in Algona, Iowa, Pharmacists Mutual Insurance Group is a specialty insurer serving healthcare professionals across all 50 states and the District of Columbia. PM Healthcare Protect combines professional liability coverage with business insurance products such as general liability and commercial property insurance, providing healthcare businesses with a single source for coverage.

item.title: Rennert Agrees to $150 Million Settlement of Peru Smelter Claims
item.description: Billionaire Ira Rennert agreed to pay $150 million to resolve more than a thousand claims that a lead smelter owned by his companies poisoned residents of a Peruvian town, reaching a deal on the eve of a U.S. Attorneys for residents of La Oroya, Peru, said they’d reached the accord just as the first set of lawsuits over the smelter’s pollution was headed to trial June 29 in St. The settlement with Rennert’s company, Doe Run Resources Corp., resolves almost a third of the suits filed by people living in the Andean highlands town who were seeking billions of dollars in damages.

item.title: Supreme Court Scales Back Roundup Cancer Lawsuits
item.description: Supreme Court reined in thousands of lawsuits pursued in state courts accusing Bayer of failing to warn users that the active ingredient in its Roundup weedkiller causes cancer, handing a major legal victory on Thursday to the German company. Related: Federal Judge Sends $7.25B Roundup Settlement Back to Missouri Court President Donald Trump’s administration backed Bayer in the case. Related: Bayer Gets Mixed Reception at Supreme Court on Roundup Suits Jackson called the ruling “remarkable and regrettable, for it unjustifiably closes the courthouse doors to state tort plaintiffs like Durnell.”

item.title: London Faces Huge Financial Cost Tied to Rising Heat, Mayor Says
item.description: London’s efforts to deal with rising temperatures come at a considerable financial cost that will require the city to turn to private investors for help, the mayor of the UK capital said. Instead of being a “future threat,” extreme heat is now “a growing reality for Londoners,” the office of London Mayor Sadiq Khan said in a statement on Thursday. Heat waves in 2022 — the year Londoners first experienced 40C — cost £1.5 billion ($2 billion), the mayor’s office said in a report.

item.title: Taiping Indonesia complete first cross-border renminbi reinsurance deal
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Singapore eyes alternative risk transfer solutions under new corporate structure
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Monetary Authority of Singapore says awaited PCC consultation is coming soon
item.description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has signalled that a long-awaited consultation on new laws to establish a Protected Cell Company (PCC) structure that could be used for collateralised reinsurance arrangements and efficient insurance-linked securities (ILS) issuance is coming soon. Of course, regulatory moves can take time and now the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has encouragingly signalled again that the PCC structure remains a strategic goal, with a consultation process now set to be launched to gain feedback on the rules it might require and what the reinsurance and ILS industry actually need from it. In a speech given at a dinner in Singapore yesterday, Gan Kim Yong, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry, and Chairman of the Monetary Authority of Singapore, highlighted the PCC plans as he discussed the need for capital markets participation in insurance and Singapore’s ambitions to capture more of that business.

item.title: Aon appoints Chin Fong Teh as Chief of Staff, Asia Pacific, Reinsurance Solutions
item.description: Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions, part of broking group Aon, has announced that Chin Fong Teh has been appointed Chief of Staff, Asia Pacific, based in Singapore, reporting to Rupert Moore, Chief Executive Officer, Asia Pacific, Reinsurance Solutions at Aon. In his new role, he will work with senior leaders within Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions team to support strategic priorities and contribute to the development of operational effectiveness across the Asia Pacific region, with a continued focus on client outcomes. Prior to joining Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions, Fong Teh held senior regional operational positions and was involved in large-scale transformation and integration initiatives, alongside experience in broking.

item.title: CFC adds affirmative AI coverage
item.description: Responding to customer demand, specialist insurance provider CFC has launched a programme of updates to embed affirmative artificial intelligence (AI) coverage within its portfolio. By implementing explicit AI-related language across seven key policies, this rollout provides clients with certainty on the handling of AI related risks, moving away from reliance on implied or silent coverage. Rather than relying on implied or silent coverage, we see value in being explicit about how AI is treated.” The updates are designed to eliminate ambiguity, fostering more consistent claims outcomes.

item.title: UK businesses increasingly exposed as flood preparedness declines: Previsico
item.description: Previsico noted that it highlights a “deepening paradox” where awareness of flood risk is increasing, but preparedness has fallen sharply, leaving businesses, insurers, and asset holders dangerously exposed. A new report from flood forecasting firm Previsico, based on insights from more than 70 UK organisational leaders, has found a sharp decline in flood preparedness despite rising awareness of risk. “And alarmingly, 20.93% of respondents noted that uncertainty around future flood risk made investment a challenge, suggesting that further education and increased availability of tailored data are necessary to help organisations to understand potential risk,” Previsico added.
